customer_addr:
P. O. BOX 1198
Pilot Point, TX 76258

citations.narrative:
While I inspected this facility on 4-9-2026, the licensee, Scott Edwards, made numerous harassing statements towards
me and Animal Care, with a final statement during the exit meeting that, “if USDA ever comes back, they had better bring
a gun”. At the beginning of the inspection Mr. Edwards walked up to me and I introduced myself, and I said I was there for
a routine inspection and a complaint regarding two lions. Mr. Edwards stated that he did not appreciate surprises and did
not appreciate USDA at his facility. I remarked that I needed to complete the inspection and talk about the complaint
regarding the lions. The facility manager, Mr. Edwards and I then walked to the lion enclosure, where two lions were
exhibiting a severe loss of coordination, stumbling, and falling. Mr. Edwards stated that he spends thousands of dollars on
veterinary care. While discussing the lions, Mr. Edwards said I was not interested in the lions, but only my paycheck. Mr.
Edwards asked me if I worked for Iran. Mr. Edwards became agitated and left the inspection. I conducted the remainder of
the inspection of animals with Mr. Wornick. After viewing the animals and facilities, I asked to review records. The records
were in the office of Mr. Edwards. During records review I asked questions regarding the program of veterinary care. Mr.
Edwards again asked if I worked for Iran and stated his belief that he controlled USDA. He said I could write what I
wanted and that he would tie everything up in court for at least 10 years and by then he would be deceased so it would
not matter. He continued to make remarks about veterinarians because USDA only wanted a paycheck and that my only
intent was for money and not animal care. When I asked again when the lions had been seen by the AV, Mr. Edwards
stated they were seen often enough. When I asked about current treatment, he stated that he was a lion expert and that
the lions were having a good life and all USDA wanted to do was surprise him. He stated that we have soldiers at war and
dying for our country and how did I feel about them dying and me being there. He continually raised his voice, becoming
more and more argumentative. I informed him that he needed to be respectful and asked him to lower his voice. He
continued to raise his voice and repeat the statements about his disregard for USDA. I left the office briefly to call the
facility’s veterinarians. I then went back to Mr. Edwards to conduct the exit briefing. Mr. Edwards became very agitated
and repeated the remarks about USDA, and I informed him that another inspection would be done soon to follow up on
the direct veterinary care citation. At that time Mr. Edwards stated, “if USDA ever comes back, they had better bring a
gun”. I immediately left the facility and called my supervisor.
To be corrected from the date of this inspection forward.
An exit interview was not able to be conducted to review this citation. This is a continuation of the report for the routine
inspection conducted 4-9-26 and contains the remaining non-compliances.
n
